published_prior_art.cite: schroder2025conformal + tibshirani2019covariateshift: standard causal/covariate-shift change of measure
none: no located result states this exact finite-list graded-tail modulus
tibshirani2019covariateshift: weighted conformal coverage; jin2023robustconformal: robust conformal coverage under partially identified shifts
jin2023robustconformal, cauchois2020robustvalidation, and kiyani2024lengthoptimization do not state the certified-tail connected-interval frontier

qiu2023unknownshift supplies the unknown-shift impossibility, while kiyani2024lengthoptimization, yang2024drcalibration, and schroder2025conformal do not give this fixed-certificate heavy-tail regret frontier

accepted_scope_caveats.label: technical_internal_limitation / ass:certificate-containment
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The finite ratio list, its induced envelope, and containment of the actual ratio are externally certified rather than estimated from the calibration sample.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: All coverage and both frontier questions are conditional on a fixed externally certified finite list containing w.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: State a concrete shell-richness condition with both lower and upper shell mass, then propose the resulting population lower-order expression explicitly.
Add a finite-support worked certificate with K>1 that computes r_w and r_rob, has nonzero ordinary-cell width, and verifies the tail-envelope accounting.
For the sample frontier, formulate a generic certified-ratio-list class and compare the final rate against both robust conformal inference and CPL rather than only their individual settings.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 with the conditional fixed-certificate scope; prioritize a nondegenerate shell construction and a matched regret rate.
